domain: Persian astrolabe planispheric projection (conformal stereographic projection of 3D celestial sphere to 2D disc; interchangeable latitude-specific retes; mechanical lookup for prayer times, navigation, etc.)
specific_mechanism: Fixed conformal projection of high-dim sphere to 2D disc with task-specific interchangeable overlay templates; angle-preserving (conformal); invertible; supports many evaluation purposes from one base projection.
llm_application: ASTROLABE-EVAL: a deterministic evaluation framework that (i) STEREOGRAPHIC-PROJECTS embedding vectors (output token embeddings, attention patterns, hidden states) onto a unit disc via a FIXED angle-preserving conformal map; (ii) overlays a library of TASK-SPECIFIC TEMPLATES on the disc marking 'regions of interest' (e.g., factuality-region, hallucination-region, refusal-region); (iii) evaluates outputs by counting projection in each region. Different from UMAP/PCA/t-SNE because (a) conformal (preserves angles), (b) invertible (can recover from projection), (c) supports MULTIPLE interchangeable overlays without re-projecting. Useful for white-box mechanistic eval.
novelty_claim: Standard embedding visualization uses PCA/UMAP/t-SNE — lossy, non-conformal, single-overlay. HARUSPEX-EVAL (R359) uses discrete K-zone tuples. Spherical-LLM-embedding methods exist but don't use stereographic projection for eval. ASTROLABE-EVAL differs by CONFORMAL STEREOGRAPHIC PROJECTION + INTERCHANGEABLE OVERLAY LIBRARY + ANGLE-PRESERVING + INVERTIBLE eval surface — supporting multi-purpose deterministic eval from one base projection.
